item.title: Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10) 2026: Apply Now for Equality Now Legal Empowerment Programme (Deadline: 16 June 2026)
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10) is a prestigious international legal training programme designed to strengthen the capacity of lawyers and legal professionals working on women’s rights, gender equality, and strategic human rights litigation. Organised by the Bulgarian Gender Research Foundation (BGRF) in partnership with Equality Now, this 10th round of WHRTI continues a […]

The post Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10) 2026: Apply Now for Equality Now Legal Empowerment Programme (Deadline: 16 June 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10) is a prestigious international legal training programme designed to strengthen the capacity of lawyers and legal professionals working on women’s rights, gender equality, and strategic human rights litigation. Organised by the Bulgarian Gender Research Foundation (BGRF) in partnership with Equality Now, this 10th round of WHRTI continues a long-standing legacy of empowering legal experts to advance justice for women and girls globally.

As part of Equality Now’s Equal Justice Academy, the programme builds on more than 20 years of impactful training, advocacy, and legal capacity development focused on gender equality and women’s rights protection.

This hybrid training programme is scheduled for 2026 and combines online learning with an intensive in-person legal training session in Bulgaria.

About the Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10)

The WHRTI is a specialised legal education and professional development initiative launched in 2004. It is designed to equip young and mid-career legal professionals with the knowledge, tools, and practical experience required to litigate women’s rights cases and advocate for gender justice at national and international levels.

The programme places strong emphasis on building legal expertise in:

  • Strategic litigation
  • Human rights law
  • Gender equality frameworks
  • Advocacy and legal reform

It is particularly focused on strengthening legal capacity in Europe, especially Central and Eastern Europe (CEE) and Newly Independent States (NIS), while also accepting a limited number of participants from other regions.

Core Focus Areas of WHRTI-10

Participants will receive advanced training across multiple critical areas affecting women’s rights globally. These include:

1. Gender-Based Violence and Justice Systems

  • Gender-based violence against women
  • Legal frameworks addressing domestic and sexual violence
  • Survivors’ access to justice

2. Technology-Facilitated Violence

  • Online harassment and digital abuse
  • Cyber violence against women
  • Legal responses to tech-enabled gender crimes

3. Sexual and Reproductive Health Rights (SRHR)

  • Access to reproductive healthcare
  • Legal protection of bodily autonomy
  • Policy frameworks supporting SRHR

4. Economic and Social Rights

  • Employment discrimination
  • Workplace equality laws
  • Economic empowerment through legal systems

5. Political Participation and Representation

  • Women in leadership and decision-making
  • Legal barriers to political participation
  • Protection against political violence

6. Intersectional Discrimination

  • Discrimination based on race, disability, age, migration status, or socioeconomic background
  • Legal strategies for inclusive justice systems

7. Countering Backlash Against Women’s Rights

  • Anti-gender movements
  • Disinformation and misinformation targeting gender equality
  • Legal resistance strategies

Programme Structure and Training Format

The WHRTI-10 programme uses a hybrid learning model, combining digital learning with immersive in-person training.

Training Components:

  • Online Courses (July & September 2026):
    • Focus on technology-facilitated gender-based violence
    • Interactive legal learning modules
    • Case-based discussions and analysis
  • In-Person Training (November 2026):
    • 6-day intensive session in Bulgaria
    • Training in feminist legal theory
    • Exposure to international human rights mechanisms
    • Peer learning with global legal professionals

Financial Support and Benefits

Selected participants will receive full financial support, ensuring accessibility for qualified legal professionals regardless of economic background.

Programme Benefits:

  • Full tuition coverage
  • Travel costs to Bulgaria fully funded
  • Full-board accommodation during in-person training
  • Access to global legal and human rights networks
  • Advanced training in women’s rights litigation and advocacy
  • Membership in an international community of human rights lawyers

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is highly selective and targets legal professionals with demonstrated commitment to women’s rights.

Applicants must:

  • Be practising lawyers or legal experts
  • Have at least one year of affiliation with a legal advocacy institution
  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to women’s rights and gender equality
  • Be fluent in English
  • Be based primarily in Europe (especially CEE/NIS regions), with a limited number of participants from other regions

Application Process

  • Application Deadline: 16 June 2026
  • Applicants must submit the completed application form and supporting documents via email to:
    • office@bgrf.org 
    • tishevagenoveva@abv.bg

Selection Timeline:

  • Shortlisted candidates contacted by: 25 June 2026
  • Online interviews conducted after shortlisting
  • Final selection communicated after interviews

Why WHRTI-10 Matters

The WHRTI programme plays a critical role in strengthening legal systems worldwide by equipping lawyers with the tools to defend women’s rights in courts, policy spaces, and international legal forums.

It directly contributes to:

  • Stronger gender justice frameworks
  • Improved legal representation for women
  • Enhanced accountability for gender-based violations
  • Greater global collaboration among human rights lawyers

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Women’s Human Rights Training Institute (WHRTI-10) 2026: Apply Now for Equality Now Legal Empowerment Programme (Deadline: 16 June 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OHCHR Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026: Join the United Nations’ Global Human Rights Leadership Initiative for Young Changemakers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Young leaders committed to advancing human rights now have an extraordinary opportunity to contribute directly to a major United Nations initiative through the Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026, established by the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR). As part of the Global Alliance for Human Rights, the Youth4Rights Advisory Group seeks […]

The post OHCHR Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026: Join the United Nations’ Global Human Rights Leadership Initiative for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders committed to advancing human rights now have an extraordinary opportunity to contribute directly to a major United Nations initiative through the Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026, established by the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR).

As part of the Global Alliance for Human Rights, the Youth4Rights Advisory Group seeks to mobilize and empower young people from around the world to help shape the future of human rights advocacy, dialogue, policy engagement, and youth participation. This prestigious initiative recognizes that young people play a transformative role in building just, inclusive, peaceful, and rights-based societies.

The Youth4Rights Advisory Group will consist of twelve outstanding young leaders who will bring youth perspectives to global human rights discussions while helping design, promote, and strengthen the Global Alliance for Human Rights. Through this unique platform, selected members will work alongside international stakeholders, support youth engagement efforts, participate in global events, and contribute to advancing human rights worldwide.

For young advocates, entrepreneurs, innovators, activists, and changemakers seeking meaningful engagement with the United Nations human rights system, this opportunity offers an exceptional pathway to influence global conversations and help shape the future of human rights.

What is the Global Alliance for Human Rights?

The Global Alliance for Human Rights is an ambitious international initiative designed to bring together a broad range of stakeholders committed to strengthening human rights around the world.

The initiative seeks to:

  • Capture and amplify global commitment to human rights.
  • Strengthen public engagement with human rights issues.
  • Promote dialogue across generations and regions.
  • Encourage inclusive leadership.
  • Mobilize resources and partnerships.
  • Deliver concrete actions that advance human rights globally.

The Global Alliance is inspired by the enduring vision of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights and forms part of preparations leading to the Declaration’s 80th anniversary in 2028.

Its mission is to place human rights at the center of:

  • Leadership.
  • Public policy.
  • Community engagement.
  • Social development.
  • Public discourse.
  • Everyday life.

By connecting individuals, institutions, organizations, governments, and communities, the Alliance seeks to create meaningful impact across sectors and regions.

Why Young People Matter in Human Rights Leadership

OHCHR recognizes that young people are not simply future leaders—they are leaders today.

Young people contribute:

  • Innovation.
  • New perspectives.
  • Community-driven solutions.
  • Grassroots engagement.
  • Social mobilization.
  • Advocacy and awareness-building.

Addressing contemporary human rights challenges requires the direct participation of young people whose experiences, insights, and creativity can help shape effective responses.

Through the Youth4Rights Advisory Group, young leaders will have a formal mechanism to influence and strengthen the Global Alliance for Human Rights while ensuring youth voices remain central to global human rights efforts.

What is the Youth4Rights Advisory Group?

The Youth4Rights Advisory Group is a youth leadership body established under the Global Alliance for Human Rights.

The Advisory Group will:

  • Ensure meaningful youth participation in Global Alliance activities.
  • Bring youth perspectives into international human rights discussions.
  • Help design and evaluate human rights initiatives.
  • Strengthen youth engagement globally.
  • Promote awareness of human rights issues.
  • Support youth-led action and advocacy.

The Advisory Group serves as an important bridge between young people worldwide and the broader human rights ecosystem.

Composition of the Advisory Group

The Youth4Rights Advisory Group will consist of:

  • 12 young leaders.

OHCHR will ensure diversity in the selection process, taking into account:

  • Gender balance.
  • Geographic representation.
  • Cultural diversity.
  • Ethnic diversity.
  • Age diversity.
  • Varied professional and social backgrounds.

The goal is to create an inclusive group that reflects the diversity of youth worldwide.

Roles and Responsibilities of Youth4Rights Advisory Group Members

Selected members will actively contribute to the work of the Global Alliance for Human Rights.

Responsibilities include:

1. Supporting Global Alliance Initiatives

Members will contribute to:

  • Design.
  • Planning.
  • Implementation.
  • Monitoring.
  • Evaluation.

of Alliance initiatives and activities.

2. Promoting Human Rights Campaigns

Participants will support outreach efforts through:

  • Social media engagement.
  • Awareness campaigns.
  • Advocacy initiatives.
  • Public communication activities.

3. Strengthening Youth Engagement

Members will help:

  • Mobilize young people.
  • Encourage participation.
  • Expand youth networks.
  • Promote youth inclusion in human rights processes.

4. Participating in Events and Activities

Advisory Group members may participate in:

  • International meetings.
  • Human rights forums.
  • Consultations.
  • Campaign activities.
  • Dialogue sessions.
  • Youth engagement events.

5. Representing Youth Perspectives

Members will serve as important representatives of youth voices within the Global Alliance for Human Rights and related initiatives.

Time Commitment

Selected members should be prepared to dedicate approximately:

Eight hours per month

to Advisory Group activities.

This commitment allows participants to remain actively engaged while balancing their studies, careers, and other responsibilities.

Duration of the Mandate

The initial term of service will run from:

12 August 2026 – 31 December 2027

There is also the possibility of extending the mandate through:

December 2028

This provides selected members with a long-term opportunity to contribute to global human rights efforts.

Benefits of Joining the Youth4Rights Advisory Group

Although participation is voluntary, selected members gain significant professional, leadership, and networking opportunities.

Benefits include:

Direct Engagement with the United Nations

Participants will work within a major OHCHR initiative connected to global human rights efforts.

Global Leadership Experience

Members will strengthen:

  • Leadership skills.
  • Advocacy abilities.
  • Strategic thinking.
  • Communication capabilities.

International Visibility

Selected youth leaders will gain recognition through their participation in a United Nations human rights initiative.

Global Networking Opportunities

Participants will connect with:

  • Youth leaders.
  • Human rights advocates.
  • Policymakers.
  • International organizations.
  • Civil society actors.

Participation in High-Level Activities

Members may engage in events and activities linked to the Global Alliance for Human Rights.

Fully Covered Travel Costs

While members serve on a voluntary basis without remuneration, travel expenses related to participation in Global Alliance activities and events will be fully covered.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Between 18 and 28 years old as of 12 August 2026.

Experience Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • At least two years of experience advancing human rights or related causes at the local or national level.

Language Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Working-level proficiency in English.

Professional Background

The opportunity welcomes a wide range of applicants, including:

  • Human rights advocates.
  • Social entrepreneurs.
  • Innovators.
  • Community organizers.
  • Youth leaders.
  • Activists.
  • Changemakers.

Importantly, specialized human rights expertise is not mandatory.

Why This Opportunity is Unique

Unlike many youth engagement initiatives, the Youth4Rights Advisory Group provides a direct avenue for young people to influence a major international human rights initiative.

Participants will:

  • Shape global human rights discussions.
  • Promote youth perspectives.
  • Support international campaigns.
  • Contribute to meaningful action.
  • Build international partnerships.
  • Strengthen youth participation worldwide.

This makes the initiative particularly valuable for young people seeking meaningful leadership opportunities within the United Nations system.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

21 June 2026 at 23:59 CET

Late applications may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must complete the Official Application Form provided by OHCHR before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully demonstrate:

  • Their commitment to human rights.
  • Relevant leadership experience.
  • Youth engagement efforts.
  • Motivation to contribute to the Global Alliance for Human Rights.

Visit HERE to learn more about the OHCHR Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026.

Conclusion

The OHCHR Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for young leaders to contribute directly to a global United Nations human rights initiative. By joining this prestigious advisory body, participants will help ensure that youth voices remain central to international human rights discussions while strengthening their own leadership, advocacy, and global engagement skills. As the world approaches the 80th anniversary of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, the Youth4Rights Advisory Group represents an important platform for empowering a new generation of changemakers committed to building more inclusive, equitable, and rights-based societies worldwide.

Visit Opportunities for Youth (OFY) to discover more global Fellowship and UN opportunities.

The post OHCHR Youth4Rights Advisory Group 2026: Join the United Nations’ Global Human Rights Leadership Initiative for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights 2026: Join the United Nations’ Worldwide Youth Community Advancing Human Rights
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Young people around the world who are passionate about human rights now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a global movement through the Global Youth Network for Human Rights (GYNfHR), an initiative coordinated by the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR). The Global Youth Network for Human Rights […]

The post OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights 2026: Join the United Nations’ Worldwide Youth Community Advancing Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people around the world who are passionate about human rights now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a global movement through the Global Youth Network for Human Rights (GYNfHR), an initiative coordinated by the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR).

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights is designed to connect young people from every region of the world who are committed to promoting, protecting, and advancing human rights. Whether participants are experienced human rights advocates or simply interested in learning more about human rights issues, the Network offers an inclusive and accessible platform for engagement, dialogue, collaboration, and action.

Created and coordinated by the OHCHR Child and Youth Rights Unit, the Network provides young people with opportunities to contribute their perspectives to global human rights discussions, engage with United Nations initiatives, participate in consultations and campaigns, and connect with a diverse international community of youth changemakers.

Importantly, the Network is open to all young people regardless of their educational background, previous experience, organizational affiliation, or level of knowledge about human rights. The initiative aims to ensure that youth voices from all walks of life are heard and represented in conversations shaping the future of human rights globally.

About the Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR)

The Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR) is the principal United Nations entity responsible for promoting and protecting human rights worldwide.

OHCHR works to:

  • Promote universal human rights.
  • Monitor and report on human rights issues globally.
  • Support governments in implementing human rights obligations.
  • Strengthen accountability mechanisms.
  • Protect vulnerable and marginalized populations.
  • Facilitate youth participation in human rights processes.

Recognizing the important role that young people play in advancing social justice and human dignity, OHCHR has increasingly invested in youth engagement initiatives that encourage meaningful participation in human rights advocacy and policymaking.

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights is one of the flagship initiatives supporting this vision.

What is the Global Youth Network for Human Rights?

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights is a worldwide platform that brings together young people who share a commitment to human rights and social justice.

The Network was established in response to growing interest from young people who have participated in OHCHR youth engagement initiatives and expressed a desire for more continuous interaction, collaboration, and learning opportunities.

The Network seeks to:

  • Strengthen youth participation in human rights discussions.
  • Foster dialogue between young people from diverse backgrounds.
  • Create opportunities for collaboration and exchange.
  • Ensure youth perspectives inform UN human rights work.
  • Expand access to human rights engagement opportunities.
  • Reach young people who may not belong to formal organizations or existing participation structures.

Through the Network, youth are not merely observers of human rights processes—they become active contributors helping to shape discussions, policies, and initiatives.

Why the Global Youth Network for Human Rights Matters

Young people today face numerous challenges, including discrimination, inequality, climate change, conflict, digital rights concerns, unemployment, and barriers to participation.

At the same time, youth are leading some of the world’s most impactful social movements and advocating for change in their communities.

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights recognizes that:

  • Young people are key stakeholders in human rights issues.
  • Youth perspectives are essential to effective policymaking.
  • Human rights work benefits from diverse lived experiences.
  • Inclusive participation strengthens democratic processes.
  • Sustainable solutions require meaningful youth engagement.

By creating a global platform for youth participation, OHCHR ensures that young voices contribute directly to discussions affecting their futures.

What Does the Network Do?

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights serves as a dynamic platform that facilitates engagement, learning, collaboration, and advocacy.

1. Gathering Youth Perspectives

The Network regularly seeks input from young people through:

  • Surveys
  • Consultations
  • Calls for submissions
  • Questionnaires
  • Global discussions

These contributions help inform OHCHR’s work and ensure youth voices are reflected in human rights initiatives.

2. Sharing Information and Opportunities

Members receive access to:

  • Human rights resources
  • Educational materials
  • Participation opportunities
  • Consultations
  • Events
  • Campaigns
  • Youth engagement initiatives

This helps young people stay informed about developments in the human rights field.

3. Supporting Human Rights Campaigns

Network members can contribute to campaigns that raise awareness about:

  • Human rights protection
  • Equality and inclusion
  • Youth rights
  • Social justice
  • Civic participation
  • Global solidarity

4. Mobilizing Youth Participation

The Network encourages young people to engage in initiatives connected to:

  • The Global Alliance for Human Rights
  • OHCHR consultations
  • UN human rights campaigns
  • Youth-led advocacy efforts
  • International awareness initiatives

5. Supporting Youth-Led Initiatives

The Network promotes youth leadership by encouraging young people to:

  • Design local initiatives.
  • Organize awareness campaigns.
  • Conduct advocacy activities.
  • Promote human rights education.
  • Mobilize community engagement.

Key Benefits of Joining the Network

Membership in the Global Youth Network for Human Rights offers numerous advantages.

Global Human Rights Community

Participants join a worldwide network of youth committed to advancing human rights.

Direct Connection with OHCHR

Members gain access to opportunities coordinated by the United Nations human rights system.

Opportunities to Influence Discussions

Young people can contribute their perspectives to consultations and surveys that help shape human rights initiatives.

Access to Information and Resources

Members receive updates about:

  • Human rights developments
  • UN initiatives
  • Youth engagement opportunities
  • Campaigns and consultations

Flexible Participation

There is no fixed time commitment, making participation accessible to students, professionals, activists, and volunteers.

Youth Leadership Opportunities

Participants can engage in campaigns, advocacy efforts, and community-level human rights initiatives.

Inclusive and Accessible Environment

The Network welcomes individuals regardless of experience level or organizational affiliation.

Who Can Join?

One of the most unique aspects of the Network is its inclusivity.

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights is open to:

  • Students
  • Youth activists
  • Community leaders
  • Volunteers
  • Young professionals
  • Researchers
  • Human rights advocates
  • First-time participants
  • Members of youth organizations
  • Individuals not affiliated with any organization

No prior human rights experience is required.

The initiative particularly seeks to engage:

  • Marginalized youth
  • Underrepresented communities
  • Young people with limited access to participation platforms
  • Youth from diverse social, cultural, and geographic backgrounds

How the Network Works

The Network primarily operates through email-based communication.

Once registered, participants may receive:

  • Invitations to consultations.
  • Survey requests.
  • Information on campaigns.
  • Human rights resources.
  • Updates on opportunities.
  • Calls for participation.

Participation remains entirely voluntary and flexible.

Members can choose when and how they engage based on their interests and availability.

Why Young People Should Join

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with the United Nations human rights system while connecting with peers from around the world.

Participants gain:

  • Global exposure.
  • Learning opportunities.
  • Advocacy experience.
  • Access to international networks.
  • Opportunities to influence discussions.
  • Connections with fellow youth leaders.

Whether your passion lies in gender equality, climate justice, education, migration, digital rights, peacebuilding, disability rights, indigenous rights, youth participation, or broader social justice issues, the Network provides a platform to contribute meaningfully to global conversations.

No Application Deadline

The Global Youth Network for Human Rights operates as an ongoing initiative.

Interested young people can join the Network at any time.

How to Join

Young people interested in becoming part of the Network can register through the Official OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights Platform and begin receiving updates, opportunities, and invitations to participate in human rights initiatives worldwide.

Click HERE to learn more about the OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights 2026.

Conclusion

The OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights represents an important opportunity for young people to engage with the United Nations, contribute to global human rights discussions, and become part of a growing international community committed to justice, dignity, equality, and inclusion. By connecting youth from diverse backgrounds and creating pathways for meaningful participation, the Network ensures that young voices continue to shape the future of human rights worldwide. Whether you are an experienced advocate or simply beginning your human rights journey, joining the Global Youth Network for Human Rights can be the first step toward making a meaningful impact both locally and globally.

Discover more global Fellowship and UN opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).

The post OHCHR Global Youth Network for Human Rights 2026: Join the United Nations’ Worldwide Youth Community Advancing Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: King’s College London Fully Funded 3-Year PhD Studentship 2026 in Psychology: Receive £23,805 Annual Stipend Plus £3,000 Research Funding to Conduct Groundbreaking Research in the UK
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fully Funded 3-Year PhD Studentship in the Department of Psychology at King’s College London for the 2026/27 academic year. This exceptional doctoral opportunity offers aspiring researchers the chance to pursue a fully funded PhD at one of the world’s leading universities while receiving a generous annual stipend, full […]

The post King’s College London Fully Funded 3-Year PhD Studentship 2026 in Psychology: Receive £23,805 Annual Stipend Plus £3,000 Research Funding to Conduct Groundbreaking Research in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Fully Funded 3-Year PhD Studentship in the Department of Psychology at King’s College London for the 2026/27 academic year. This exceptional doctoral opportunity offers aspiring researchers the chance to pursue a fully funded PhD at one of the world’s leading universities while receiving a generous annual stipend, full tuition fee support, dedicated research funding, and valuable teaching experience.

Hosted within the renowned Department of Psychology at the Institute of Psychiatry, Psychology & Neuroscience (IoPPN), the studentship provides an outstanding platform for talented graduates interested in advancing psychological science through innovative and impactful research.

Successful candidates will join one of four cutting-edge research projects spanning food psychology, autism and gastrointestinal health, decision-making under risk, and intergenerational development.

Application Deadline: 21 June 2026 (23:59 GMT)

 

Why Study at King’s College London?

King’s College London is consistently ranked among the world’s leading universities and is internationally recognized for excellence in research, teaching, and innovation.

The university’s Institute of Psychiatry, Psychology & Neuroscience is one of the largest and most influential centers for mental health and psychological research globally. Students benefit from:

  • World-class supervision
  • Access to leading researchers
  • Cutting-edge research facilities
  • International academic networks
  • Interdisciplinary collaborations
  • Excellent career development opportunities

This studentship offers a unique opportunity to conduct high-impact research while developing advanced academic and professional skills.

 

Overview of the Fully Funded Psychology PhD Studentship

The Department of Psychology is seeking one outstanding PhD candidate to undertake research in one of four pre-defined doctoral projects.

The studentship will begin on:

Start Date: 1 October 2026

The successful applicant will be enrolled in a full-time PhD program lasting three years.

 

Financial Benefits

This fully funded doctoral scholarship provides substantial financial support throughout the duration of the PhD.

Annual Stipend

The selected student will receive:

  • Annual stipend equivalent to the UK Research and Innovation (UKRI/RCUK) doctoral stipend rate.
  • Current stipend value: £23,805 per year, including London weighting.
  • Stipend subject to annual updates.

Full Tuition Fee Coverage

The studentship covers:

  • Full tuition fees for Home students.
  • PhD registration costs for the entire three-year duration.

Research and Travel Funding

In addition to the stipend, students will receive:

  • Up to £3,000 annually for:
    • Research activities
    • Conference attendance
    • Fieldwork expenses
    • Academic travel
    • Project-related costs

Over three years, this could amount to:

  • Up to £9,000 in additional research support

 

Available PhD Research Projects

Applicants must apply for one of the following projects.

Project 1 (FC01): Food and the Self

Supervisor Contact:
Dr Francesca Cotier

This innovative project investigates the relationship between food, eating behaviors, and personal identity.

Research Areas

The project will:

  • Examine how food identity has been conceptualized across psychological literature.
  • Investigate how food choices contribute to self-concept and identity formation.
  • Explore lived experiences related to food and identity.

Research Methods

Students will:

  • Conduct systematic reviews.
  • Carry out qualitative interviews.
  • Develop psychological assessment tools.
  • Validate new measures of food identity.

Potential Impact

Findings could contribute to:

  • Better understanding of eating behavior.
  • Improved public health interventions.
  • More culturally sensitive nutrition and health programs.

 

Project 2 (JF02): Disorders of Gut-Brain Interaction (DGBI) in Autistic Adults

Supervisor Contact:
Dr James Findon

This project focuses on gastrointestinal symptoms among autistic adults using a disorders of gut-brain interaction framework.

Research Activities

The project will involve:

  • Reviewing existing scientific evidence.
  • Analyzing secondary datasets.
  • Comparing autistic and non-autistic adults.
  • Examining healthcare utilization patterns.

Key Variables

Research will explore:

  • Anxiety
  • Interoception
  • Sensory processing
  • Neurodevelopmental mechanisms

Expected Outcomes

The study aims to:

  • Improve understanding of gastrointestinal conditions in autism.
  • Inform clinical practice.
  • Support better healthcare interventions.

 

Project 3 (CH03): Described and Experienced Risk in Decision-Making

Supervisor Contact:
Dr Claire Heard

This project examines how individuals make decisions when information about risk is both explicitly provided and learned through experience.

Research Questions

The study investigates:

  • How people combine described and experienced information.
  • How risk perceptions evolve.
  • Which factors influence decision quality.

Research Approach

Using experimental methods, the project will assess:

  • Timing of information
  • Sequencing effects
  • Information completeness
  • Behavioral responses

Real-World Applications

Results could inform:

  • Consumer behavior research
  • Marketing strategies
  • Financial decision-making
  • Online review systems
  • Public communication campaigns

 

Project 4 (SY04): Intergenerational Play

Supervisor Contact:
Dr Sumeyra Yalcintas

This project explores the differences between parent-child and grandparent-child interactions and their effects on development and wellbeing.

Research Components

The project includes:

  • Video-recorded play sessions
  • Behavioral coding analysis
  • Questionnaires
  • Interviews with families

Areas of Investigation

Researchers will examine:

  • Interaction patterns
  • Child developmental outcomes
  • Family relationships
  • Grandparent wellbeing

Importance of the Research

The findings may provide valuable insights into:

  • Child development
  • Family psychology
  • Healthy ageing
  • Intergenerational relationships

 

Teaching and Professional Development Opportunities

An important component of the studentship involves serving as a Graduate Teaching Assistant (GTA).

Responsibilities may include:

  • Leading seminars
  • Supporting practical classes
  • Marking coursework
  • Providing feedback to undergraduate students
  • Supporting the BSc Psychology programme

These teaching duties provide valuable academic experience for students considering careers in:

  • Higher education
  • Research
  • Academic leadership

 

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following minimum academic requirements:

Standard Entry Route

  • Bachelor’s degree with 2:1 Honours classification (or overseas equivalent).

Alternative Entry Route

Applicants with:

  • Bachelor’s degree with 2:2 Honours
  • Plus a Master’s degree with Merit

may also be considered.

 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Academic Transcripts

Official academic transcripts must accompany the application where applicable.

  1. Qualification Documents

Copies of degree certificates and qualifications.

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Including:

  • Educational background
  • Employment history
  • Research experience
  • Publications (if any)
  1. Personal Statement

Applicants must explain:

  • Why they are interested in the project.
  • Their academic interests.
  • Their research experience.
  • Their motivation for pursuing a PhD.

The personal statement must clearly identify the selected project code (e.g., FC01, JF02, CH03, or SY04).

  1. Teaching Experience Information

Applicants should include any:

  • Teaching experience
  • Tutoring experience
  • Academic mentoring activities
  1. Academic Reference

One academic reference is required.

References must:

  • Be submitted from an institutional email address.
  • Meet the specified submission timeline.

 

Important Application Instructions

Applicants should:

  1. Apply through the King’s College London admissions portal.
  2. Select:
    • Department of Psychology Research MPhil/PhD (Full-Time); or
    • Department of Psychology Research MPhil/PhD (Part-Time).
  3. Tick Box 5 in the Funding Section.
  4. Include the project reference code.
  5. Skip the Research Proposal section because the projects have already been predefined.

Applicants are encouraged to contact supervisors for additional information before submitting their applications.

 

Selection Process

Following the application deadline:

Stage 1: Application Review

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Research experience
  • Project alignment
  • Academic potential

Stage 2: Interview

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to:

  • Online interview; or
  • In-person interview

Stage 3: Final Selection

Selection will consider:

  • Academic merit
  • Research capability
  • Motivation
  • References
  • Fit with the project

Successful applicants will be informed after the completion of the selection process.

 

Key Funding Information

  • Funding Type: Bench Fees, Research Training & Support Grant, Stipend, Tuition Fee Support
  • Number of Awards: 1
  • Study Level: Postgraduate Research (PhD)
  • Academic Year: 2026/27
  • Funding Code: AC11147
  • Duration: 3 Years
  • Start Date: 1 October 2026

 

Application Deadline

Deadline: 21 June 2026 at 23:59 GMT

Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered.

For prospective psychology researchers seeking a fully funded PhD opportunity at one of the world’s leading universities, this studentship offers exceptional financial support, world-class supervision, extensive research opportunities, and a pathway toward an impactful academic or professional career.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post King’s College London Fully Funded 3-Year PhD Studentship 2026 in Psychology: Receive £23,805 Annual Stipend Plus £3,000 Research Funding to Conduct Groundbreaking Research in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026: Fully Remote U.S. Government Fellowship for Researchers, Policy Experts, and Evaluators to Drive Evidence-Based Change Across Federal Agencies
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Office of Evaluation Sciences (OES), housed within the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA), is now accepting applications for its highly competitive OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026. This prestigious one-year fellowship offers researchers, evaluators, academics, nonprofit professionals, and government experts a unique opportunity to work directly on some of the most important policy challenges facing the […]

The post OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026: Fully Remote U.S. Government Fellowship for Researchers, Policy Experts, and Evaluators to Drive Evidence-Based Change Across Federal Agencies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Office of Evaluation Sciences (OES), housed within the U.S. General Services Administration (GSA), is now accepting applications for its highly competitive OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026. This prestigious one-year fellowship offers researchers, evaluators, academics, nonprofit professionals, and government experts a unique opportunity to work directly on some of the most important policy challenges facing the United States.

Beginning in Fall 2026, selected fellows will join a team of interdisciplinary experts dedicated to helping federal agencies build and use evidence to improve government programs, increase efficiency, strengthen accountability, and deliver better outcomes for millions of people.

Unlike traditional academic fellowships, the OES Evaluation Fellowship places participants at the center of real-world policymaking, where they design and implement large-scale evaluations, collaborate with senior government leaders, and apply cutting-edge social science methods to improve public services.

Application Deadline: June 24, 2026 (11:59 PM ET)

 

About the Office of Evaluation Sciences (OES)

The Office of Evaluation Sciences is a specialized team within the U.S. General Services Administration that partners with federal agencies to generate actionable evidence and improve government performance.

OES helps agencies answer critical questions such as:

  • Which government programs are most effective?
  • How can services be improved for citizens?
  • What interventions produce measurable outcomes?
  • How can public resources be used more efficiently?
  • What evidence should guide future policy decisions?

The organization brings together experts from a variety of disciplines, including:

  • Economics
  • Psychology
  • Behavioral Science
  • Public Policy
  • Statistics
  • Data Science
  • Evaluation Research
  • Sociology

Through rigorous scientific methods and evidence-based approaches, OES supports government agencies in making better-informed decisions that positively impact millions of Americans.

 

Why the OES Evaluation Fellowship Stands Out

The OES Fellowship is unlike many traditional research fellowships because fellows work directly on real government challenges while applying rigorous scientific evaluation methods.

Since its establishment, OES has:

  • Completed more than 130 government collaborations.
  • Conducted over 100 impact evaluations.
  • Worked with dozens of federal agencies.
  • Influenced policies affecting millions of people.
  • Developed innovative approaches to evidence-based governance.

The fellowship combines the intellectual rigor of academic research with the practical impact of public service.

 

Fellowship Overview

Selected fellows will join OES as Evaluators and collaborate with federal agencies to improve programs through evidence generation and policy evaluation.

The fellowship will begin in Fall 2026 and generally lasts one year, although candidates interested in serving longer may have opportunities to continue their work.

Importantly, all fellowship positions are:

  • Fully remote
  • Location-flexible within the United States
  • Embedded in high-impact federal projects
  • Focused on evidence generation and policy improvement

This arrangement allows fellows to contribute meaningfully while maintaining flexibility in where they work.

 

What Fellows Will Do

Fellows serve as Evaluators and work directly with government agencies to address important policy questions.

Key responsibilities may include:

  1. Designing Impact Evaluations

Fellows develop rigorous evaluation frameworks that help determine whether government programs are achieving intended outcomes.

Activities include:

  • Developing research questions
  • Designing evaluation methodologies
  • Building measurement frameworks
  • Establishing causal inference strategies
  1. Implementing Evidence-Based Interventions

Participants help agencies test and implement interventions designed to improve outcomes.

Examples include:

  • Increasing participation in government programs
  • Improving service delivery
  • Enhancing program efficiency
  • Supporting vulnerable populations
  1. Data Analysis

Fellows use large-scale administrative datasets to:

  • Measure program effectiveness
  • Identify policy improvements
  • Generate actionable insights
  • Inform decision-making
  1. Policy Collaboration

Participants work closely with:

  • Federal agency leaders
  • Program managers
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Behavioral scientists

This direct engagement ensures that evidence is translated into practical action.

  1. Publications and Knowledge Sharing

Fellows contribute to:

  • Academic publications
  • Policy reports
  • Evaluation summaries
  • Government guidance documents
  • Evidence-building resources

 

Real-World Impact of OES Projects

The Office of Evaluation Sciences has partnered with agencies across the federal government to tackle major challenges such as:

Government Efficiency

Projects focus on helping agencies deliver better services while maximizing public resources.

Improper Payment Reduction

OES works to identify methods that reduce errors and improve accountability in government spending.

Veteran Support

Research collaborations help improve services and outcomes for veterans.

Public Program Improvement

Evidence generated through OES collaborations helps agencies redesign and strengthen programs for greater impact.

International and Domestic Policy Challenges

OES applies behavioral science and rigorous evaluation methods to address complex issues both in the United States and abroad.

 

Fellowship Structure

For Academic and Nonprofit Professionals

Individuals from universities, research institutions, nonprofits, and government organizations may participate through the Intergovernmental Personnel Act (IPA) Mobility Program.

Under this model:

  • Fellows remain affiliated with their home institution.
  • Participants join OES on loan.
  • Appointments may be reimbursable or non-reimbursable.

Previous fellows have come from institutions such as:

  • Harvard University
  • Reed College
  • Miami University
  • University of California San Diego

For Federal Employees

Federal employees may join through:

  • Temporary detail assignments
  • Inter-Agency Agreements (IAA)
  • Rotational opportunities
  • Presidential Management Fellow rotations

Previous participants have joined from agencies including:

  • U.S. Department of Labor
  • U.S. Forest Service
  • National Science Foundation
  • U.S. Department of the Treasury
  • U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
  • U.S. Department of Agriculture

 

Benefits of the OES Evaluation Fellowship

Successful fellows will gain access to numerous professional benefits, including:

Professional Development

  • Hands-on experience with federal policymaking
  • Exposure to large-scale impact evaluations
  • Advanced evidence-building expertise
  • Opportunities to shape national policies

Research Opportunities

  • Conduct rigorous evaluations
  • Work with large administrative datasets
  • Publish research findings
  • Apply leading social science methods

Networking and Collaboration

  • Collaborate with senior government leaders
  • Work alongside interdisciplinary experts
  • Engage with federal agencies across government
  • Build a national professional network

Career Advancement

The fellowship provides a highly respected credential for careers in:

  • Public policy
  • Government leadership
  • Academia
  • Evaluation research
  • Behavioral science
  • Data science
  • International development
  • Program management

 

Why Researchers Should Consider This Fellowship

The OES Evaluation Fellowship offers a rare combination of:

  • Academic rigor
  • Policy influence
  • National-scale impact
  • Professional flexibility
  • Cross-sector collaboration

Few fellowships provide the opportunity to directly influence federal programs while simultaneously advancing research and evidence generation.

Participants gain experience that is highly valued across government, academia, think tanks, foundations, and nonprofit organizations.

 

How to Apply

Applicants must submit their applications through the official OES application portal.

The application process includes:

  1. Completing the online application form.
  2. Submitting required supporting materials.
  3. Providing a writing sample.
  4. Demonstrating qualifications and relevant experience.

Applicants unable to access the online form may request a PDF application by contacting OES directly via email.

 

Application Deadline

Deadline: June 24, 2026

Applications must be submitted no later than 11:59 PM Eastern Time (ET) on Wednesday, June 24, 2026.

Late applications will not be considered.

 

Final Thoughts

The OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for researchers, evaluators, academics, nonprofit professionals, and government experts who want to use evidence to improve public policy and government effectiveness.

By joining the Office of Evaluation Sciences, fellows gain unparalleled access to federal decision-making processes, contribute to high-impact evaluations affecting millions of people, and help shape the future of evidence-based governance in the United States.

For professionals seeking a fellowship that combines research excellence, public service, and measurable societal impact, the OES Evaluation Fellowship stands among the most influential opportunities available in 2026.

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post OES Evaluation Fellowship 2026: Fully Remote U.S. Government Fellowship for Researchers, Policy Experts, and Evaluators to Drive Evidence-Based Change Across Federal Agencies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations University Consultant Researcher Position 2026 in New York, USA: Earn $110,004 Per Year Plus Health Insurance Support While Shaping the Future of Global Governance
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The United Nations University (UNU) has announced an outstanding international consultancy opportunity for experienced researchers and policy professionals who are passionate about global governance, multilateral cooperation, international development, and United Nations policy processes. The position, titled Consultant: Researcher, Multilateralism and Global Governance, is hosted by the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research in New […]

The post United Nations University Consultant Researcher Position 2026 in New York, USA: Earn $110,004 Per Year Plus Health Insurance Support While Shaping the Future of Global Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations University (UNU) has announced an outstanding international consultancy opportunity for experienced researchers and policy professionals who are passionate about global governance, multilateral cooperation, international development, and United Nations policy processes.

The position, titled Consultant: Researcher, Multilateralism and Global Governance, is hosted by the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research in New York, United States. This prestigious 16-month consultancy contract offers an annual remuneration of USD 110,004, monthly payments of USD 9,167, additional health insurance support, and the opportunity to contribute directly to innovative solutions addressing some of the world’s most pressing global challenges.

This role is ideal for experienced researchers and policy professionals seeking to work at the intersection of global governance, multilateral cooperation, sustainable development, and international policymaking.

Application Deadline: 25 June 2026

 

About the United Nations University (UNU)

The United Nations University is the academic and research arm of the United Nations system. For more than five decades, UNU has produced policy-relevant research and generated innovative solutions to complex global issues affecting countries around the world.

As a global think tank and postgraduate teaching institution, UNU focuses on:

  • Sustainable development
  • Human security
  • Economic development
  • Global governance
  • International cooperation
  • Capacity development
  • Knowledge dissemination

Through its network of research institutes located across multiple countries, UNU contributes to advancing the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) while supporting evidence-based policymaking at international, regional, and national levels.

 

About UNU Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR)

The United Nations University Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) serves as one of the key research institutes within the UNU system.

Headquartered in New York with an office in Geneva, UNU-CPR produces policy-focused research and strategic thought leadership aimed at helping the United Nations and its Member States address urgent global challenges.

The Centre focuses on:

  • Strengthening multilateral cooperation
  • Promoting innovative policy solutions
  • Supporting UN reform initiatives
  • Building resilience within the international system
  • Facilitating strategic policy dialogue

 

About the Global Governance Innovation Platform (GGI)

The selected researcher will join the Global Governance Innovation Platform (GGI), a major multi-year initiative designed to help UN Member States create innovative multilateral mechanisms capable of addressing emerging global challenges.

The project examines nearly 80 years of multilateral cooperation and institutional development to identify successful governance innovations that can inform future global institutions.

The platform provides:

  • Interactive data visualizations
  • Historical governance analysis
  • Institutional innovation case studies
  • Policy guidance for Member States
  • Research tools supporting institution-building efforts

GGI seeks to demonstrate that the international community has repeatedly developed creative governance solutions and that these lessons can help address today’s increasingly complex global problems.

 

Position Overview

The successful candidate will work from UNU-CPR’s New York office on a hybrid schedule and contribute significantly to the Global Governance Innovation Platform.

The researcher will:

  • Conduct cutting-edge policy research
  • Analyze governance innovations worldwide
  • Organize strategic convenings
  • Lead policy briefings
  • Engage with policymakers
  • Produce influential publications
  • Develop governance case studies
  • Support international partnerships

This is a highly visible position involving interaction with senior policymakers, diplomats, experts, and UN stakeholders.

 

Main Responsibilities

  1. Research and Data Collection

The researcher will:

  • Develop new research topics relevant to GGI priorities
  • Identify key policy processes and negotiations
  • Analyze governance innovations across sectors and regions
  • Produce original governance case studies
  • Validate research findings with project partners
  • Conduct interviews for media assessments
  • Support donor-driven project implementation
  1. Publications and Knowledge Products

The consultant will be responsible for producing high-quality research outputs, including:

  • Long-form thematic research reports
  • Policy briefs
  • Strategic analyses
  • Media articles
  • Knowledge products
  • Training materials for online learning programs

The researcher will also commission expert policy papers aligned with donor priorities.

  1. Public Engagement and Strategic Partnerships

A major component of the role involves engagement with policymakers and stakeholders.

Responsibilities include:

  • Designing engagement strategies
  • Strengthening Member State participation
  • Representing GGI at international forums
  • Participating in expert panels
  • Contributing to media discussions
  • Expanding partnerships with experts and regional organizations
  1. Team Leadership and Coordination

The researcher will support:

  • Recruitment of fellows
  • Recruitment of consultants
  • Recruitment of interns
  • Supervision and mentoring activities
  • Communication and outreach initiatives
  1. Management and Administrative Support

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Supporting project management activities
  • Assisting UNU-CPR leadership
  • Ensuring quality assurance processes
  • Supporting administrative functions
  • Contributing to project effectiveness

 

Required Qualifications

Educational Requirements

Applicants should possess:

  • A PhD in a relevant discipline (strongly preferred)

Relevant disciplines include:

  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Economics
  • Law
  • International Development
  • Public Policy
  • Related fields

Candidates without a PhD may still be considered if they possess:

  • An advanced degree
  • Strong publication records
  • Significant professional experience

 

Professional Experience

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least 5 years of relevant experience
  • Strong policy research expertise
  • Experience working on global governance issues
  • Knowledge of UN institutions and operations
  • Understanding of intergovernmental processes
  • Experience translating research into policy recommendations

Additional advantages include:

  • Data visualization experience
  • Event convening experience
  • Stakeholder engagement expertise
  • Media communication experience

 

Language Requirements

Candidates must possess:

  • Professional fluency in English

An additional UN official language will be considered an asset.

 

Desired Competencies

UNU seeks candidates who demonstrate:

Core Values

  • Inclusion
  • Integrity
  • Humility
  • Humanity

Professional Behaviours

  • Collaboration
  • Strategic thinking
  • Analytical excellence
  • Results orientation
  • Continuous learning
  • Innovation and adaptability

 

Salary and Benefits

The consultancy package includes:

Financial Benefits

  • Annual remuneration: USD 110,004
  • Monthly salary: USD 9,167
  • Additional health insurance contribution: USD 12,000 over the contract period

Professional Benefits

  • Work with the United Nations system
  • Engage directly with policymakers and diplomats
  • Contribute to global governance reform
  • Build an international professional network
  • Participate in high-level policy discussions
  • Gain visibility through publications and international events

International Mobility Benefits

  • Potential G-4 visa sponsorship
  • UN-funded mission travel when required
  • Exposure to global policy processes
  • Collaboration with international experts

 

Contract Duration

The selected consultant will receive:

  • A 16-month Consultant Contract (CTC)
  • Expected start date by 1 September 2026
  • Possible earlier start in August 2026

The role will be conducted primarily in person in New York with hybrid work arrangements.

 

Travel Support

Where official missions are required, UNU will cover:

  • Economy-class airfare
  • Accommodation costs
  • Meals and incidentals within UN-approved rates

Travel will require:

  • Medical clearance
  • Security clearance
  • Completion of BSAFE training
  • Appropriate visa documentation

 

How to Apply

Applicants must:

  1. Submit an application through the UNU online application portal.
  2. Complete the official UNU P11 Form.
  3. Provide a motivation statement within the application.
  4. Answer position-specific screening questions.

Candidates should ensure all required documents are completed accurately before submission.

 

Application Deadline

Deadline: Thursday, 25 June 2026

Applications must be received no later than 12:00 PM New York Time. Late submissions will not be considered.

 

Why You Should Apply

This consultancy offers a rare opportunity to influence global policymaking while working with one of the most respected research institutions within the United Nations system.

Beyond the highly competitive compensation package, successful candidates will contribute to shaping future international institutions, strengthening multilateral cooperation, and helping governments develop innovative solutions to global challenges.

For experienced researchers seeking a career at the forefront of international policy and governance, this position represents one of the most attractive UN consultancy opportunities available in 2026.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post United Nations University Consultant Researcher Position 2026 in New York, USA: Earn $110,004 Per Year Plus Health Insurance Support While Shaping the Future of Global Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Dovetail Impact Foundation Hiring Investment Director (International): Lead Transformational Investments Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Professionals with a passion for fighting extreme poverty through strategic philanthropy and social impact investing now have an exceptional opportunity to join Dovetail Impact Foundation as an Investment Director, International. Based in Africa, this influential leadership position offers the opportunity to identify, support, and accompany extraordinary nonprofit and social enterprise leaders working in some of […]

The post Dovetail Impact Foundation Hiring Investment Director (International): Lead Transformational Investments Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals with a passion for fighting extreme poverty through strategic philanthropy and social impact investing now have an exceptional opportunity to join Dovetail Impact Foundation as an Investment Director, International.

Based in Africa, this influential leadership position offers the opportunity to identify, support, and accompany extraordinary nonprofit and social enterprise leaders working in some of the world’s most challenging environments. The successful candidate will help cultivate a portfolio of high-impact organizations and provide the strategic capital, guidance, and partnership needed to accelerate their growth and impact.

For experienced leaders in venture philanthropy, social impact investing, nonprofit leadership, and development finance, this role presents a unique opportunity to help transform lives while shaping the future of mission-driven organizations serving people experiencing extreme poverty.

About Dovetail Impact Foundation

Dovetail Impact Foundation exists to fuel extraordinary organizations that serve people living in extreme poverty.

The Foundation partners with nonprofit organizations and social enterprises that are addressing poverty and injustice with integrity, innovation, and scalable solutions. Rather than simply providing funding, Dovetail works closely alongside its partners, offering a combination of:

  • Capital
  • Strategic support
  • Capacity building
  • Thought partnership
  • Long-term accompaniment

The Foundation describes itself as a high-touch funder that seeks to walk alongside leaders with humility, care, and excellence.

Its mission is rooted in supporting organizations that are creating meaningful and lasting change for vulnerable communities around the world.

Position Overview

Position Title

Investment Director, International

Organization

Dovetail Impact Foundation

Reporting To

Chief Operations Officer

Location

Africa (West Africa Preferred)

Employment Requirement

Applicants must already possess legal authorization to live and work in an African country.

Visa sponsorship is not available.

The Opportunity

The Investment Director will oversee a strategic portfolio of international nonprofit and social enterprise partners, helping them scale impact and maximize outcomes for people experiencing extreme poverty.

This role combines:

  • Strategic philanthropy
  • Investment management
  • Due diligence
  • Portfolio leadership
  • Relationship management
  • Ecosystem building
  • Thought partnership

The successful candidate will serve as both an investor and trusted advisor to organizational leaders working on the frontlines of poverty alleviation.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic Portfolio Management

The Investment Director will:

  • Build a portfolio of high-impact venture-stage grant partners.
  • Identify organizations where catalytic capital can unlock significant growth.
  • Occasionally evaluate impact investment opportunities.
  • Manage a portfolio of more than 25 strategic partners.
  • Ensure investments align with Dovetail’s mission, values, and strategy.

2. Investment Assessment and Due Diligence

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting rigorous organizational assessments.
  • Evaluating growth potential.
  • Reviewing organizational strategy and leadership.
  • Assessing financial sustainability.
  • Making high-conviction investment recommendations.

The Foundation seeks professionals with exceptional judgment and strong pattern-recognition abilities.

3. Partner Accompaniment and Support

Beyond funding decisions, the Investment Director will:

  • Serve as a trusted advisor to portfolio organizations.
  • Provide strategic thought partnership.
  • Support organizational growth and scaling efforts.
  • Help leaders navigate critical moments in their development journey.
  • Strengthen organizational capacity and effectiveness.

4. Strategic Learning and Ecosystem Development

Working alongside the Director of Strategic Learning and Insights, the successful candidate will:

  • Identify emerging leaders and organizations.
  • Detect opportunities for systems-level impact.
  • Develop deep sector expertise.
  • Analyze trends and opportunities across focus areas.

5. Relationship Building

The role requires cultivating relationships with:

  • Peer funders
  • Philanthropic organizations
  • Development experts
  • Social enterprise leaders
  • Nonprofit executives
  • Strategic partners

These relationships will support partner growth and strengthen funding opportunities.

6. Foundation Representation

The Investment Director will represent Dovetail Impact Foundation in:

  • Conferences
  • Strategic gatherings
  • Partner meetings
  • Philanthropic networks
  • Funder coalitions
  • Industry events

The role will help advance the Foundation’s reputation and influence within the global social impact ecosystem.

Ideal Candidate Profile

Dovetail is seeking a leader who demonstrates:

Mission Alignment

  • Deep commitment to serving people experiencing extreme poverty.
  • Passion for supporting early-stage and growth-stage organizations.
  • Strong belief in human flourishing and social impact.

Leadership Qualities

  • Exceptional judgment.
  • High emotional intelligence.
  • Strong self-awareness.
  • Excellent self-management.
  • Humility and curiosity.
  • Commitment to continuous learning.

Relationship Excellence

Candidates should be known for:

  • Building trust quickly.
  • Managing complex relationships effectively.
  • Providing valuable advice during critical moments.
  • Exercising patience and wisdom under pressure.

Strategic Thinking

The ideal candidate can:

  • Connect seemingly unrelated information.
  • Recognize emerging patterns.
  • Identify leverage points for impact.
  • Make informed decisions despite uncertainty.

Travel Requirements

The position includes approximately:

  • 20% travel commitment.
  • 3–4 international trips annually.

This travel supports portfolio management, partner engagement, and representation of the Foundation.

Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

Experience

  • 8+ years of relevant professional experience.
  • Experience in venture philanthropy, social impact investing, or organizational leadership.
  • International and cross-cultural experience.

Education

Preferred qualifications include:

  • MBA
  • Master’s degree
  • Other advanced degree in a relevant field

Technical Skills

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Financial analysis expertise.
  • Ability to interpret organizational financial statements.
  • Understanding of operational efficiency and cost structures.
  • Investment assessment skills.

Language Skills

Strong preference will be given to candidates who are fluent in:

  • French (spoken and written)

Faith-Based Requirements

As a Christian grantmaking foundation, Dovetail asks applicants to:

  • Identify as followers of Jesus Christ.
  • Affirm the Apostles’ Creed and Nicene Creed.
  • Demonstrate commitment to Christian values and service.
  • Support broad and diverse communities regardless of faith background.

The Foundation emphasizes service, humility, and love for neighbors as central aspects of its organizational culture.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This is more than a traditional investment role.

The successful candidate will have the opportunity to:

  • Influence organizations serving some of the world’s most vulnerable communities.
  • Help scale innovative poverty-alleviation solutions.
  • Partner closely with visionary nonprofit and social enterprise leaders.
  • Shape strategic philanthropy across Africa and beyond.
  • Join a mission-driven organization focused on long-term transformational impact.

For experienced professionals seeking a career that combines investment expertise, social impact, faith-based leadership, and global development, this role offers an extraordinary opportunity to make a lasting difference.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for global job opportunities

 

The post Dovetail Impact Foundation Hiring Investment Director (International): Lead Transformational Investments Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Asian Development Bank Hiring Sustainable Solutions Specialist for the Pacific Region: Accelerate Climate Action and Low-Carbon Development
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Professionals passionate about climate action, sustainable development, and resilience-building now have an exciting opportunity to work with one of the world’s leading development institutions. The Asian Development Bank (ADB) is recruiting a Sustainable Solutions Specialist to support climate change initiatives and low-carbon development efforts across the Pacific region. This strategic international position offers the opportunity […]

The post Asian Development Bank Hiring Sustainable Solutions Specialist for the Pacific Region: Accelerate Climate Action and Low-Carbon Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals passionate about climate action, sustainable development, and resilience-building now have an exciting opportunity to work with one of the world’s leading development institutions. The Asian Development Bank (ADB) is recruiting a Sustainable Solutions Specialist to support climate change initiatives and low-carbon development efforts across the Pacific region.

This strategic international position offers the opportunity to influence climate policy, mobilize climate finance, strengthen resilience, and support sustainable development across Pacific developing member countries while working from ADB Headquarters in Manila, Philippines.

If you are an experienced climate and sustainable development professional looking to make a meaningful impact on regional development, this role could be the next major step in your career.

About the Asian Development Bank (ADB)

The Asian Development Bank is a leading multilateral development bank committed to fostering sustainable, inclusive, and resilient growth across Asia and the Pacific.

Founded in 1966 and headquartered in Manila, Philippines, ADB works with governments, development partners, and stakeholders to address some of the region’s most pressing challenges, including:

  • Climate change
  • Poverty reduction
  • Economic development
  • Sustainable infrastructure
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Disaster risk management
  • Social inclusion

Through innovative financing, technical expertise, and strategic partnerships, ADB supports transformative projects that improve lives and strengthen economies throughout the Asia-Pacific region.

Position Overview

Job Title

Sustainable Solutions Specialist

Reference Number

260573

Department

Pacific Department (PARD)

Division

Operations Coordination Unit (PAOC)

Duty Station

ADB Headquarters, Manila, Philippines

Position Grade

TI1

Employment Type

Fixed-term appointment with an initial contract of up to three years and the possibility of renewal.

Application Deadline

Deadline: June 19, 2026 (11:59 PM Manila Time / 0800 GMT)

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the closing date through the ADB Career and Employment System (ACES).

Why This Role Matters

Climate change poses significant risks to many Pacific nations, including rising sea levels, extreme weather events, environmental degradation, and economic vulnerabilities.

ADB is seeking a highly qualified specialist who can help accelerate:

  • Low-carbon development
  • Climate resilience
  • Adaptation planning
  • Sustainable investment
  • Disaster risk management
  • Climate finance mobilization

The successful candidate will support governments and institutions in developing and implementing climate solutions that contribute to long-term sustainable growth and resilience.

Key Responsibilities

The Sustainable Solutions Specialist will play a central role in advancing climate priorities across Pacific developing member countries.

Key responsibilities include:

1. Climate Policy and Strategy Development

  • Support climate-related policies, programs, and strategies.
  • Strengthen integration of climate change and environmental priorities into country partnership strategies.
  • Promote holistic and systemic approaches to climate and disaster risk management.

2. Stakeholder Engagement and Partnerships

  • Build partnerships with:
    • Government agencies
    • Climate institutions
    • Development partners
    • Environmental organizations
    • Sector stakeholders
  • Lead policy dialogue and collaboration efforts.

3. Climate Policy Analysis

Analyze and assess key policy frameworks, including:

  • Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs)
  • Long-Term Strategies (LTS)
  • Green Growth Strategies
  • Low-Carbon Development Plans
  • National Adaptation Plans
  • Other climate and environmental policies

The analysis will help identify investment priorities and opportunities across multiple sectors.

4. Climate Risk and Adaptation Support

The specialist will:

  • Develop climate risk assessments
  • Produce technical materials
  • Support adaptation planning
  • Strengthen climate resilience investments

5. Climate Finance Mobilization

A significant aspect of the role involves helping secure climate finance by:

  • Identifying funding opportunities
  • Developing funding proposals
  • Supporting due diligence processes
  • Mobilizing internal and external climate finance resources

6. Project Development and Management

The successful candidate will contribute to:

  • Loan projects
  • Grant-funded initiatives
  • Technical assistance projects
  • Climate-focused investment programs

These projects will support sustainable development across Pacific member countries.

7. Monitoring and Reporting

Responsibilities include:

  • Tracking climate targets
  • Monitoring outcomes
  • Identifying implementation gaps
  • Sharing lessons learned and best practices
  • Supporting strategic climate approaches at the country level

8. Knowledge Sharing and Representation

The specialist will represent the Pacific Department in:

  • Working groups
  • Climate-focused seminars
  • Cross-departmental meetings
  • Regional and international events

Qualifications and Experience Required

To be considered for this position, applicants should possess:

Educational Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in:
    • Sustainable Development
    • Climate Change
    • Economics
    • Public Policy
    • Environment
    • Related disciplines
  • Postgraduate qualifications are highly desirable.

Professional Experience

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • At least six years of relevant experience.
  • Experience in climate change and sustainable development.
  • International experience working across several countries.
  • Experience in Pacific developing member countries.

Technical Expertise

Strong knowledge and experience in:

  • Climate adaptation
  • Climate risk assessment
  • Environmental policy
  • Climate finance
  • Low-carbon technologies
  • Sustainable development planning

Communication and Leadership

Candidates should have:

  • Excellent written and verbal English skills.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement abilities.
  • Experience communicating complex technical and policy information.
  • Team leadership and management experience.

Benefits and Compensation

ADB offers a highly competitive international benefits package designed to support staff and their families.

Benefits may include:

Financial and Relocation Benefits

  • Competitive salary package
  • Relocation support
  • Housing allowance
  • Education allowance

Health and Well-being

  • Comprehensive medical coverage
  • Health benefits
  • Insurance plans
  • Life insurance

Retirement and Leave

  • Retirement plan
  • Paid annual leave
  • Parental leave benefits

Professional Development

  • Continuous learning opportunities
  • Leadership development
  • International exposure
  • Career growth within a leading development institution

Relocation to Manila

The successful candidate will be based at ADB Headquarters in Manila, Philippines.

ADB will cover reasonable relocation expenses, making this an excellent opportunity for qualified international candidates seeking global development experience.

Why You Should Apply

This role offers a rare opportunity to influence climate policy and sustainable development at a regional scale.

Professionals selected for this position will:

  • Contribute to climate resilience across Pacific nations.
  • Support low-carbon development strategies.
  • Mobilize climate finance.
  • Engage with governments and international partners.
  • Shape sustainable development outcomes for millions of people.
  • Work with one of the world’s most respected multilateral development institutions.

Click here for more and to apply

For experienced climate and sustainable development professionals seeking meaningful global impact, this opportunity represents an exceptional career pathway.

Click here for more global job opportunities
