The post Asian Development Bank Hiring Sustainable Solutions Specialist for the Pacific Region: Accelerate Climate Action and Low-Carbon Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Pathways Impact Fund Opens New $7.5 Million Grant Opportunity for Regional Organizations Advancing Student Career Pathways
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Regional organizations working to transform educational and career outcomes for students now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Pathways Impact Fund. Catalyzed by StriveTogether, the Fund has announced a new grant opportunity that will distribute a total of $7.5 million to regional intermediary organizations dedicated to advancing purposeful pathways that help […]

The post Pathways Impact Fund Opens New $7.5 Million Grant Opportunity for Regional Organizations Advancing Student Career Pathways appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Regional organizations working to transform educational and career outcomes for students now have an exciting opportunity to secure significant funding through the Pathways Impact Fund. Catalyzed by StriveTogether, the Fund has announced a new grant opportunity that will distribute a total of $7.5 million to regional intermediary organizations dedicated to advancing purposeful pathways that help students access high-quality educational and career opportunities.

This funding initiative reflects a growing national commitment to ensuring that young people are equipped with the knowledge, skills, experiences, and support systems needed to thrive in today’s economy. Through substantial multi-year investments, the Pathways Impact Fund aims to strengthen regional leadership, expand student access to effective pathways programs, and drive long-term economic mobility for future generations.

What Is the Pathways Impact Fund?

The Pathways Impact Fund is a national funding initiative focused on helping regions build and scale systems that connect education, workforce development, employers, and community stakeholders around a shared vision for student success.

The Fund is grounded in the belief that every young person should have access to purposeful pathways that prepare them for meaningful careers, economic mobility, and long-term personal fulfillment. By investing in regional organizations that coordinate these efforts, the Fund seeks to accelerate systems-level change and create measurable impacts for students across communities.

Funding Available

The Pathways Impact Fund will provide grants ranging from:

  • $900,000 to $1.5 million per organization
  • Distributed over a three-year funding period

In total, the Fund will award $7.5 million to selected regional intermediary organizations.

This substantial investment is designed to help organizations expand and strengthen the core components of purposeful pathways, ultimately increasing student participation and success.

Beyond Funding: Additional Benefits for Grantees

Selected organizations will receive much more than financial support.

Successful grantees will gain access to:

1. National Learning Network

Organizations will become part of a nationwide network of leaders committed to improving student pathways and outcomes. This network creates opportunities for peer learning, collaboration, and shared problem-solving.

2. Tailored Support

Recipients will receive customized assistance designed to strengthen implementation strategies, address challenges, and maximize impact.

3. Community of Practice

Grantees will participate in a collaborative environment where regional leaders can exchange insights, best practices, innovations, and lessons learned.

4. Capacity to Scale Student Impact

The funding is specifically intended to help organizations expand access to purposeful pathways and reach more students over the next three years.

The Vision Behind Purposeful Pathways

Purposeful pathways are structured systems that help students successfully transition from education into careers and economic opportunities.

The Pathways Impact Fund focuses on supporting pathways that integrate three essential components:

Accelerated Coursework

Students gain access to advanced learning opportunities that prepare them for higher education and workforce success.

Career-Connected Learning

Students engage in meaningful experiences that connect classroom learning with real-world careers and industries.

High-Quality Advising

Students receive personalized guidance to help them navigate educational choices, career planning, and future opportunities.

Together, these components help create a stronger bridge between education and employment while promoting economic mobility.

Who Can Apply?

The funding opportunity is specifically designed for place-based regional or state intermediary organizations that play a leadership role in coordinating pathways efforts within their communities.

Eligible applicants must be directly embedded in and accountable to the communities they serve.

Examples of eligible organizations include:

  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Regional collaboratives
  • Public-private partnerships
  • Workforce boards
  • Postsecondary institutions
  • Similar intermediary organizations

Organizations must demonstrate active leadership in bringing together partners across education, workforce, government, and employer sectors.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for a Pathways Impact Fund grant, applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

Organizational Requirements

  • Operate as a regional or state intermediary organization.
  • Be place-based and community-embedded.
  • Demonstrate accountability to local communities.
  • Not operate solely as a national intermediary supporting regions remotely.

Partnership Requirements

Applicants must actively convene and coordinate partners across:

  • K–12 education systems
  • Postsecondary institutions
  • Workforce organizations
  • Employers
  • Government agencies

Governance Requirements

Organizations must possess formal governance documentation, including examples such as:

  • Regional strategic plans
  • Charters
  • Formal partnership agreements

Programmatic Requirements

Organizations must currently support all three core pathways components:

  1. Accelerated coursework
  2. Career-connected learning
  3. High-quality advising

These components must be implemented in partnership with K–12 school districts.

Growth Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate a credible strategy for scaling pathways access and reaching additional students during the three-year grant period.

Application Process

The Pathways Impact Fund application process includes multiple phases.

Phase One: Screener and Letter of Interest (LOI)

Interested organizations must first complete a brief eligibility screener.

Eligible applicants will then receive access to the full Letter of Interest form.

Deadline: July 10

Phase Two: Full Application (Invitation Only)

Organizations selected to move forward after the Letter of Interest stage will be invited to submit a full application.

Deadline: August 28

Click here for more and to apply

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across the United States, communities are increasingly recognizing the importance of creating stronger connections between education, workforce development, and economic opportunity.

The Pathways Impact Fund seeks to support regional leaders who are already driving this work and help them scale their impact to serve more students effectively.

Through strategic investments, collaborative learning, and systems-level support, the Fund aims to ensure that more young people can access pathways that lead to successful careers, economic mobility, and fulfilling futures.

Organizations that are committed to advancing purposeful pathways and expanding opportunities for students are encouraged to begin the application process before the July 10 Letter of Interest deadline.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

 

The post Pathways Impact Fund Opens New $7.5 Million Grant Opportunity for Regional Organizations Advancing Student Career Pathways appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AU-EU Youth Voices Grants 2026: Up to €10,000 for Youth-Led Organizations and Groups Across Africa and Europe
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Young people across Africa and Europe are being invited to transform their ideas into meaningful action through the AU-EU Youth Voices Third-Party Funding Mechanism (TPFM) 2026. Designed to support youth-led organizations and groups, this funding opportunity provides grants ranging from EUR 1,000 to EUR 10,000 to implement projects that strengthen youth participation, advocacy, accountability, and […]

The post AU-EU Youth Voices Grants 2026: Up to €10,000 for Youth-Led Organizations and Groups Across Africa and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people across Africa and Europe are being invited to transform their ideas into meaningful action through the AU-EU Youth Voices Third-Party Funding Mechanism (TPFM) 2026. Designed to support youth-led organizations and groups, this funding opportunity provides grants ranging from EUR 1,000 to EUR 10,000 to implement projects that strengthen youth participation, advocacy, accountability, and community engagement.

The funding mechanism is part of the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective, an ambitious initiative working to build more inclusive, youth-responsive societies across Africa and Europe. Through this programme, young people are not only encouraged to identify challenges affecting their communities but are also provided with the resources and support needed to develop practical solutions and influence broader policy discussions.

For youth-led groups seeking funding to implement local projects with regional, continental, or cross-continental significance, this opportunity offers a valuable platform to create sustainable impact while amplifying the voices of young people.

About the AU-EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective

The AU-EU Youth Voices Lab is a collaborative initiative that aims to strengthen youth participation in decision-making processes across Africa and Europe.

The programme recognizes that young people are experts in their own realities and should play a central role in shaping policies, programmes, and solutions that affect their lives.

Through funding, advocacy support, capacity building, and dialogue opportunities, the initiative seeks to:

  • Strengthen youth leadership.
  • Promote youth participation in governance.
  • Amplify the voices of marginalized and hard-to-reach young people.
  • Encourage accountability and civic engagement.
  • Foster cooperation between youth in Africa and Europe.
  • Create pathways for youth-led policy influence.

The Third-Party Funding Mechanism serves as one of the key tools for achieving these objectives.

What is the Third-Party Funding Mechanism (TPFM)?

The Third-Party Funding Mechanism is a grant programme designed specifically for youth-led groups and organizations working on issues that matter to young people.

Rather than imposing predetermined solutions, the mechanism empowers youth groups to identify local challenges and develop their own community-driven responses.

The funding supports initiatives that:

  • Address local youth priorities.
  • Strengthen youth participation.
  • Promote youth rights.
  • Increase accountability.
  • Encourage policy dialogue.
  • Support advocacy efforts.
  • Build stronger and more inclusive communities.

Importantly, funded projects are expected to contribute not only to local communities but also to broader discussions taking place at national, regional, continental, and AU-EU levels.

Grant Amount

Selected youth-led groups can receive funding ranging from:

  • Minimum Grant: EUR 1,000
  • Maximum Grant: EUR 10,000

The amount awarded will depend on the scale, feasibility, impact potential, and relevance of the proposed activities.

What Types of Activities Can Be Funded?

The programme supports a wide range of youth-led activities aimed at creating positive change.

Eligible activities include:

1. Workshops and Trainings

Projects that strengthen skills, leadership, civic participation, advocacy, entrepreneurship, or community engagement among young people.

2. Youth-Led Research

Research initiatives that generate evidence on issues affecting youth and contribute to policy discussions.

3. Advocacy Campaigns

Activities designed to influence decision-makers and promote youth-centered policies and reforms.

4. Public Events and Dialogues

Community forums, consultations, discussions, and engagement platforms bringing together youth and stakeholders.

5. Community Consultations

Projects that gather youth perspectives and strengthen community participation in decision-making.

6. Publications and Reports

Development of assessments, policy briefs, reports, and research publications.

7. Digital and Printed Awareness Materials

Communication products designed to inform, educate, and mobilize young people.

8. Accountability and Participation Initiatives

Projects that improve transparency, governance, accountability, and civic engagement.

Priority Themes and Areas of Interest

The funding mechanism welcomes projects addressing a broad range of youth-related issues, including:

  • Youth participation
  • Youth rights
  • Accountability and governance
  • Policy dialogue
  • Social inclusion
  • Gender equality
  • Climate justice
  • Education
  • Employment and livelihoods
  • Peacebuilding
  • Civic engagement
  • Community development
  • Youth empowerment

The programme is intentionally flexible to allow young people to propose solutions that reflect their local realities.

Who Can Apply?

The funding opportunity is open to youth-led groups and organizations working with and for young people aged 15–35.

Applicants must be based in one of the programme’s intervention countries.

Eligible Countries

Europe

  • Belgium

Africa

  • Burkina Faso
  • Burundi
  • Cameroon
  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Nigeria
  • Senegal
  • Sierra Leone
  • South Sudan
  • Tanzania
  • Tunisia
  • Uganda
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

Only applicants operating within these countries are eligible to apply.

What Makes a Strong Application?

The programme seeks youth-led groups that demonstrate:

  • Strong community connections.
  • Clear understanding of local challenges.
  • Practical and achievable project ideas.
  • Commitment to youth participation.
  • Inclusive approaches.
  • Ethical and safe project implementation.
  • Potential for meaningful impact.

Applications should clearly explain:

  • The problem being addressed.
  • Why it matters.
  • Who will benefit.
  • Planned activities.
  • Expected outcomes.
  • Budget allocation.

How the Selection Process Works

The AU-EU Youth Voices Third-Party Funding Mechanism follows a structured selection process.

Step 1: Call for Proposals

A funding call is launched, outlining eligibility requirements, funding priorities, required documents, and deadlines.

Step 2: Application Submission

Youth-led groups submit their project ideas and funding requests.

Step 3: Review Process

Applications are evaluated based on:

  • Relevance.
  • Community impact.
  • Feasibility.
  • Innovation.
  • Alignment with programme goals.
  • Organizational capacity.

Step 4: Additional Verification

Shortlisted applicants may undergo additional checks regarding governance, safeguarding, financial management, and implementation capacity.

Step 5: Grant Agreement

Successful applicants sign a grant agreement outlining responsibilities, reporting requirements, budgets, and implementation timelines.

Step 6: Project Implementation

Selected groups implement their activities within their communities.

Step 7: Ongoing Support

Grantees may receive:

  • Coaching.
  • Technical guidance.
  • Reporting support.
  • Communication assistance.
  • Learning and reflection support.

Step 8: Results Sharing

Grantees document outcomes, lessons learned, achievements, and impact stories.

2026 Funding Cycle

The second edition of the Third-Party Funding Mechanism officially opened on 8 June 2026.

This cycle aims to support youth-led organizations in delivering impactful projects that contribute to youth participation, empowerment, and advocacy.

Applicant Support Webinar

To help applicants prepare strong proposals, an online support webinar will be held on:

19 June 2026

The webinar will provide:

  • Application guidance.
  • Funding information.
  • Eligibility clarification.
  • Question-and-answer sessions.
  • Proposal development tips.

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to participate.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity goes beyond financial support.

Successful applicants gain:

  • Funding to implement youth-led ideas.
  • Increased visibility.
  • Opportunities to influence policy dialogue.
  • Access to broader AU-EU youth networks.
  • Capacity-building support.
  • Mentorship and guidance.
  • Opportunities to amplify youth voices.
  • Recognition as youth leaders driving change.

For organizations committed to creating meaningful impact for young people, the AU-EU Youth Voices Third-Party Funding Mechanism offers a powerful opportunity to transform ideas into action.

Application Deadline

29 June 2026 at 23:59 EAT

Applicants are encouraged to review the Funding Manual carefully before applying and submit their proposals well before the deadline.

How to Apply

Interested youth-led groups should:

  1. Review the Funding Manual.
  2. Confirm eligibility.
  3. Develop a strong project proposal.
  4. Complete the Online Application Form.
  5. Submit all required information before the deadline.

For inquiries, applicants can contact:

au-euyvlfund@restlessdevelopment.org

Click HERE to learn more about the AU-EU Youth Voices Grants 2026.

Conclusion

The AU-EU Youth Voices Third-Party Funding Mechanism 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for youth-led organizations and groups across Africa and Europe to transform innovative ideas into meaningful action. By providing grants of up to €10,000, alongside technical support and capacity-building opportunities, the programme empowers young people to address pressing community challenges, strengthen youth participation, and contribute to policy dialogue at local, national, continental, and cross-continental levels.

Whether your organization is working on youth rights, climate justice, education, employment, gender equality, peacebuilding, accountability, or civic engagement, this funding mechanism offers the resources needed to amplify youth voices and create lasting impact. Beyond financial support, successful applicants become part of a broader movement committed to building more inclusive, responsive, and youth-centered societies across Africa and Europe.

If your youth-led group has a strong vision for change and a commitment to serving young people in your community, now is the time to act. Carefully review the Funding Manual, participate in the support webinar, and submit a compelling proposal before the 29 June 2026 deadline. This could be your opportunity to turn local solutions into transformative action while contributing to a stronger and more connected youth movement across two continents.

Visit Opportunities for Youth (OFY) to discover more global Grant, African, and European opportunities.

The post AU-EU Youth Voices Grants 2026: Up to €10,000 for Youth-Led Organizations and Groups Across Africa and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
